ORDER DENYING DEFENDANT DANA COMPANIES, LLC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT DANA COMPANIES, LLC (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS DANA CORPORATION)'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT MR. PEARSON WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. MR. PEARSON'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. AS TO DEFENDANT'S SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR ARGUMENT, DEFENDANT SUSTAINED ITS INITIAL BURDEN. DR. BRUCH'S DECLARATION CREATES A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER MR. PEARSON'S EXPOSURE TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT WAS A SUBSTANTIAL FACTOR IN CAUSING OR CONTRIBUTING TO THE DEVELOPMENT OF HIS ASBESTOS-RELATED DISEASE. DEFENDANT'S OBJECTIONS TO DR. BRUCH'S DECLARATION ARE OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: TERI L. JACKSON, REPORTER: PATRICIA RODRIGUEZ OF AIKEN WELCH, CSR #12436

HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFFS' CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFFS DO NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WITH THE REQUISITE MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO WARRANT THE IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. PLAINTIFFS' RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDES DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. HOWEVER, EVEN IF DEFENDANT HAD SUSTAINED ITS BURDEN, PLAINTIFFS SUBMITTED EVIDENCE CREATING A TRIABLE ISSUE WHETHER DEFENDANT ACTED WITH THE REQUISITE MALICE, FRAUD OR OPPRESSION TO WARRANT THE IMPOSITION OF PUNITIVE DAMAGES. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Union Carbide's motion for summary adjudication is denied as to issues 1-4 and 6 (sophisticated intermediary defense, bulk supplier defense and punitive damages) and taken off calendar as to issue 5 (false representation). The sophisticated intermediary defense does not shield defendant from liability. (Stewart). Defendant failed to present evidence showing what knowledge Hamilton Materials had about the hazards of asbestos and what warnings, if any, it provided to Hamilton Materials. The bulk supplier defense is not available to a supplier of raw asbestos in asbestos exposure cases because asbestos is inherently dangerous and raw asbestos fibers do not change when they become a component of an asbestos-containing product. (Garza; Jenkins; Arena). As to punitive damages, there are triable issues whether defendant made misrepresentations about and/or withheld material information concerning its knowledge of the hazards of asbestos and thus whether it acted in conscious disregard for the safety of persons who were exposed to its asbestos. Plaintiff represents that he will dismiss his cause of action for false representation, although the court sees no dismissal on file. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)
